name: Mushroom, Bechamel, and Cheese Pizzette
recipeInstructions:
1 Preheat the oven to 500 degrees for at least 30 minutes.
2 Lightly coat a baking sheet with vegetable oil.
3 Clean mushrooms and slice thin, then saute them in 2 tablespoons of butter until all their excess liquid has evaporated.
4 Divide 1 recipe pizza dough into 6 or 10 or 12 equal pieces and roll or press out each ball to the desired size.
5 Arrange the pizzettes on one or more prepared baking sheets. Spread each pizzette with some Bechamel Sauce.
6 Distribute the sauted mushrooms over the sauce and top with the grated Gruyere cheese and Parmesan cheese.
7 Dot each pizzette with 2 tablespoons unsalted butter.
8 Bake for 10 to 15 minutes, or until golden.
9 Yield: 6 6-inch appetizers size OR 10 to 12 4-inch party size pizzette.
Variations:
- Soak 2 ounces of porcini in boiling water to cover for 10 minutes. Rinse them well to rid them of their sand. Chop them roughly and saute them in 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter for 3 minutes. Add the mushrooms to the Bechamel and proceed with the recipe.
- Fresh golden oak (also known as shiitake mushrooms) or other wild mushrooms can be substituted for cultivated mushrooms. Halve the amount indicated in the recipe and prepare them in the same manner as regular mushrooms.
